module.name: cases
module.description: A piecewise function with a big brace — "f(x) = { value if condition, … }". Body = one branch per section, "value | condition".
module.aiSpec:
A PIECEWISE / cases definition with a large brace, e.g. an absolute value or a step function. Block module: split the body with `<!-- @ -->`, one branch per section written `value | condition` (both are Markdown, so use \( \) for math). This is a lightweight layout; for a full equation use a KaTeX \begin{cases} block.
Body: one `value | condition` per section. Either side may contain \( \) math.
Parameters: - name (text, default f(x)): the left-hand side, rendered as LaTeX (do not add \( \) yourself). - delimiter (select: brace | bracket | none, default brace): the big grouping symbol — { , [ , or none. - condcolor (color, default blank = muted grey): color of the condition column. - colgap (number px, default 20): horizontal gap between the value and condition columns (4–80). - bracesize (CSS length, default 2.6em): font size of the delimiter, e.g. 3em.
Usage: <!-- @cases name: |x|, delimiter: brace, colgap: 28 --> x | \(x \ge 0\) <!-- @ --> -x | \(x < 0\) <!-- @end -->

param.name: name
param.label: Left-hand side (LaTeX)
param.desc: Left-hand side, rendered as LaTeX; no \( \) needed.

param.name: colgap
param.label: Column gap (px)
param.desc: Gap between value and condition columns.

param.name: bracesize
param.label: Delimiter size
param.desc: CSS font size of the delimiter, e.g. 3em.

snippet.label: Piecewise (cases)
snippet.description: Piecewise function; each section is "value | condition".
